message:
feat(extensions): add JS extension entry-point discovery and resolution system

Implement a comprehensive file discovery pipeline for JavaScript/TypeScript
extensions loaded from npm packages and local directories. This enables
pi to automatically locate extension entry files from package.json
declarations, workspace layouts, and conventional directory structures
without requiring explicit file paths.

Key components:

- resolve_extension_entry_file(): Resolves a path to a supported JS/TS
  entry file, trying all known extensions (ts, tsx, js, mjs, cjs, mts,
  cts) when no extension is specified

- collect_extension_entries_from_dir(): Scans a directory for index.*
  files, standalone entry files, and nested package directories following
  Node.js resolution conventions

- is_likely_auxiliary_extension_entry(): Heuristic classifier that
  inspects file names and content (up to 32KB preview) for extension
  registration patterns (registerCommand, registerTool, etc.)

- discover_auxiliary_example_entries(): Finds additional extension files
  in examples/demos directories, capped at 24 entries per package

- parse_pi_extensions_from_package(): Reads the "pi.extensions" field
  from package.json (supports both string and array values)

- resolve_package_declared_entries(): Resolves declared extension paths
  including node_modules cross-references via workspace package lookup

- find_package_json_ancestors(): Walks up the directory tree collecting
  all ancestor package.json files for workspace root detection

- discover_and_resolve_extensions_from_package(): Top-level orchestrator
  that combines package.json parsing, entry resolution, auxiliary
  discovery, and bundle clustering (capped at 40 cluster directories)
  into a final deduplicated list of extension entry points

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
